questions.question: Why are games the proving ground for every major RL breakthrough?
options: Game companies fund the research

Clean win/loss reward, infinite self-play episodes, the game IS the simulator (perfect model), tractable action spaces, and multi-agent structure forcing adversarial robustness — from TD-Gammon (1992) through AlphaZero to DeepSeek-R1 (2025)
Games have the largest datasets
Human evaluation is easy on games
questions.explanation: The lesson's unifying lens: self-play + search + policy improvement. Each landmark generalizes the previous, ending with GRPO applying the recipe to text reasoning.

questions.question: What is AlphaZero's core training loop?
options: Supervised learning on grandmaster games

Self-play games where MCTS (guided by the policy-value net's priors via PUCT selection) produces visit distributions that become POLICY TARGETS — the net trains supervised on its own search output: L = (v−z)² − π·log p + reg
Q-learning over board positions
Evolutionary tournament selection
questions.explanation: Search improves the raw policy; the policy then makes the search stronger. Zero human knowledge — one recipe mastered chess, shogi, and Go after tens of millions of self-play games.

questions.question: What does MuZero remove from AlphaZero's requirements?
options: The need for any neural network

Known rules: it learns a latent dynamics model (h encodes observations, g predicts next latent state + reward, f outputs policy and value) and runs MCTS entirely in the LEARNED latent space — one algorithm spanning Go, chess, shogi, AND Atari
The self-play stage
The value function
questions.explanation: Schrittwieser 2019. Stochastic MuZero (2022) added chance nodes for backgammon-class games; Muesli and Gumbel MuZero improved sample efficiency.

questions.question: How does GRPO map the AlphaZero recipe onto LLM reasoning?
options: It trains LLMs to play chess

The "game" is answering a math/coding problem, tokens are actions, and the win signal comes from a VERIFIER (test passes, answer matches) — the same self-play-and-improve loop with group-relative baselines replacing the critic
It uses MCTS over chess openings
It replaces RL with supervised learning
questions.explanation: DeepSeek-R1 (2024-2025) is the latest entry in the games lineage: verifiable rewards play the role win/loss played for AlphaZero — clean, ungameable, infinitely repeatable.

questions.question: How does MCTS's PUCT rule balance exploration and exploitation?
options: It always picks the highest Q-value
a* = argmax Q(s,a) + c·p(a|s)·√N(s)/(1+N(s,a)) — current value estimate plus a bonus that's high for moves the policy prior likes but the search hasn't visited much
It samples actions uniformly
It uses ε-greedy with decaying ε
questions.explanation: The prior p focuses search where the net expects promise; the visit-count denominator forces breadth. The resulting visit distribution is the improved policy the net then learns to imitate.
